_comment: Add your own content here. Keep this as valid JSON: comments are stored in keys that begin with _comment because GitHub Pages serves plain JSON.

site._comment: Browser title and search description. Leave blank until you are ready to publish real content.
site.title: Abhishek Karve

profile._comment: Top profile block. Use paths like assets/profile.jpg for local files, or full https URLs.

links._comment: Repeat this object for CV, Scholar, GitHub, LinkedIn, or other profile links. Delete it if unused.
links.label: CV

sections._comment: Simple text section, useful for an About section. Set visible to false to hide without deleting.
sections.title: About Me
sections.intro: Applied Physics PhD Student at Stanford. Interested in applications of Atomic Molecular and Optical (AMO) Physics to Quantum Information Science (QIS).

parts: I am a research assistant in the
working on building the next generation quantum transducer using a Rydberg Rb85 atom ensemble in a hybrid millimeter-wave and optical cavity.

sections._comment: Research experience section. Use title for lab/group/company, meta for institution/location, detail for role, note for dates, and bullets for project details.
sections.title: Research Experience

items.title: Simon Lab
bullets: Building a next-generation quantum transducer using a Rydberg Rb85 atom ensemble in a hybrid millimeter-wave and optical cavity.

items.title: Covey Lab
bullets: Designed the vacuum chamber assembly for a new quantum computing experiment with modular transportable atom arrays.
Implemented an AOM-based setup for focusing a beam through the AOM to achieve high switching speeds.
Constructed a multi-level system simulation to look at qubit dynamics under Rydberg pulses.
Built printed circuit boards to isolate digital and analog signals for experiment control for a new Ytterbium QIS experiment.
